title: peracarpa carnosa var. circaeoides
intro: Peracarpa carnosa var. circaeoides, also known as the "clove pimpernel," is a flowering plant species native to Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, thanks to its health benefits such as relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and promoting digestion. Herbal preparations of Peracarpa carnosa var. circaeoides include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and ointments for topical application.
